mnemonics: HUMAN-CENTRED A.I. = Have Users Monitor And Navigate - Control, Ethics, Norms, Trust, Reliability, Explainability, Design - Accountability, Impact
keyPoints: The framework has two core dimensions: Level of Automation (from human-led to fully autonomous) and Impact on Human Welfare (from low-stakes to critical).
Its purpose is to guide designers in making intentional choices about an AI system's role, ensuring it augments rather than replaces human capability.
The most critical systems require the highest levels of human oversight, transparency, and robustness, as defined by their position in the high-impact, high-automation quadrant.
commonMistakes: Assuming full automation is always the goal; the framework argues the appropriate level depends entirely on the potential impact.
Focusing only on the technical 'automation' dimension while neglecting the ethical 'impact' dimension.
Misjudging the potential impact of a system, leading to under-preparation for real-world risks and consequences.
